items.reason: site/index.html and site/docs/index.html render as styled HTML with navigation; deterministic check confirms no href to .md/.yaml/.yml under site/. OpenAPI pages (site/openapi/hosted-json.html, oneroster-core.html, qti-core.html) reference Redoc per the spec-conformance check.

site/index.html has 'Platform Provides' and 'App Developer Owns' sections (populated via app.js with bullets naming OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, Open Badges/CLR and developer-owned items like pedagogy, UI quality, and data minimization). However, the bullets are injected by JavaScript at runtime rather than being present as static HTML, which weakens the 'reads as guidance' substance check for crawlers/static viewers but does function in the browser.

Each generator (scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, generate_integration_governance_core.py) reads its dictionary/*.v1.json and emits SQL comments, OpenAPI JSON, Markdown, and HTML in one run. check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies that descriptions/decision IDs match across all surfaces.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md covers OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy. Each has a corresponding dictionary file (oneroster-core.v1.json, case-core.v1.json, qti-core.v1.json, caliper-core.v1.json, integration-governance-core.v1.json — the last covers LTI/Security/Privacy) with multiple objects and many fields.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md provides per-spec deferred ledgers with reasons (e.g., 'Requires hosted auth, certification testing, pagination...', 'GitHub Pages cannot host server-side auth state'), and docs/spec-gap-backlog.md item 10 explicitly tracks 'Expand OneRoster from core demo to full OneRoster 1.2 accounting' as Partial. However, individual fields (e.g., OneRoster demographics, agents, score scales) are listed as 'covered in broader dictionary but not yet executable' rather than each having an explicit per-field sourceStandard mapping or unsupported entry, and QTI/Caliper deferred items are still at the area level rather than full field-level coverage.

scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces that every object/field/value in each dictionary has descriptions across SQL/OpenAPI/Markdown/HTML. Sampled fields (e.g., person.display_name='The name shown in user interfaces.', organization.name='The organization name people recognize.', caliper_event.action='What happened in the event.', case_item.full_statement='The complete text of the standard, competency, skill...') are written in plain language a non-engineer can understand.

demo/server.js exposes /people, /classes, /enrollments, /sql/query etc., backed by demo/schema.sql + seed via better-sqlite3. demo/test.js asserts '/organizations' returns 3 records with sourcedId fields and verifies /views/class-roster, /sql/query read-only protection. VERIFY.md runs `cd demo && npm run reset-db && npm test`.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql creates real Postgres tables and views (people, class_roster, gradebook_results) with anon SELECT policies. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms the project at qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co is loaded and verified live on 2026-04-25 via scripts/check_supabase_rest.py, which hits PostgREST endpoints /rest/v1/people, /rest/v1/class_roster, /rest/v1/gradebook_results.

site/index.html ships a browser SQL console (sql.js loading demo/schema.sql + seed.sql) plus a 'Live JSON endpoints' panel that fetches site/api/*.json. site/index.html also contains a copy-pasteable curl command against the live Supabase REST URL with the publishable key. Both execute against real data (seed in browser; live PostgREST via curl).

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md contains all ten required entries (Person identity, Class/course/section/context, Roles, Enrollment vs membership, Results/scores, Standards alignment, Identifiers, Time/session, Content/resource, Tenancy). Each names specs involved, conflict, choice, mappings table back to each spec, and tradeoff in real prose.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md contains a machine-readable `produces_fields` JSON block listing per-decision dictionary field references (e.g., DEC-001 produces person.display_name, caliper_actor.actor_type, etc.). Dictionary fields in dictionary/*.v1.json carry `decision_id` (e.g., person.display_name has decision_id 'DEC-001-person-agent-subject'). check_dictionary_artifacts.py validates the cross-references.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql enables RLS but the policies (`demo_read_organizations on public.organizations for select to anon, authenticated using (true)`) use `using (true)` with no tenant_id predicate. None of the OneRoster fact tables (organizations, people, classes, enrollments, line_items, results) have a `tenant_id` column. There is no integration test using two distinct JWTs scoped to different tenants. docs/spec-gap-backlog.md item 17 acknowledges this is 'Not done'.

dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json defines security_scope_policy objects mapping scopes to api_resource, allowed_action, privacy_ceiling, field_patterns, and allowed_roles, which is the right shape. However, individual dictionary fields in oneroster-core.v1.json, caliper-core.v1.json, etc., do not carry an `oauth_scope` attribute, and the OpenAPI documents do not declare securitySchemes referencing those scopes. The mapping exists at the policy table level but is not propagated to per-field metadata or OpenAPI security.

Every field in dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json, caliper-core.v1.json, case-core.v1.json, qti-core.v1.json, and integration-governance-core.v1.json carries a `privacy_class` value drawn from a documented vocabulary (e.g., 'operational', 'directory', 'education_record', 'behavioral', 'sensitive', 'system', 'public'). The vocabulary is enumerated in data/data-dictionary.seed.json (referenced by site/app.js as `state.dictionary.privacy_classes`).

No `audit_log` table exists in supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql and no Postgres trigger or Edge Function wraps restricted reads. dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json defines a `privacy_audit_events` schema but it is documentation-only; the table is not created in the live migration. docs/spec-gap-backlog.md item 18 confirms 'Not done'.

docs/spec-gap-backlog.md includes a 'Rubric Trace For Open Items' table that maps tenant_isolation_enforced, oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields, audit_log_for_sensitive_reads, edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints, edge_functions_propagate_user_jwt, rls_enabled_on_referenced_tables, and lead_spec_full_accounting to backlog items with status, target slice, and owner notes. However, several other rubric items that are partial/fail in this evaluation (developer_guide_present nuance, oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields per-field exposure, lead_spec_full_accounting per-field coverage) are tracked only at the area level, not against every individual rubric id.

docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md has explicit columns 'Structured source', 'Generated artifacts', 'Runnable slice', and 'Unsupported ledger' populated honestly per spec — e.g., QTI shows 'Yes / Yes / No / Yes', OneRoster core shows 'Yes / Yes / Yes / Partial', Common Cartridge shows 'No / No / No / Partial'.

The repository has no `supabase/functions/*` directories — neither the gradebook bulk submit, LTI 1.3 launch handler, Caliper event ingestion, nor OAuth token exchange exist as Edge Functions. docs/admin-operations.md states 'No service-role callers are approved yet.' docs/spec-gap-backlog.md items 20-22 confirm these slices are 'Docs only'. Both prerequisites (supabase_cli, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) are reported true in the runtime status, so this is a real fail, not blocked.
No Edge Functions exist yet (per evidence above), so per the rubric's `blocked_if` clause this item is blocked on the prerequisite covered by edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints.
blocked_prerequisites: At least one Supabase Edge Function must exist (see edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints)

items.reason: supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json shows rowsecurity=true and a SELECT policy on every OneRoster public.* table (organizations, people, classes, enrollments, line_items, results, courses, academic_sessions, source_identifiers). However, Caliper, LTI, and QTI tables referenced by their dictionaries (caliper_events, lti_launches, qti_assessment_items, etc.) have no live tables and therefore no RLS, because those slices are not yet runtime-backed. The substance check requires 'every table referenced by OneRoster, Caliper, LTI, or QTI endpoints' — only OneRoster is fully covered.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py exists and queries /rest/v1/people, /rest/v1/class_roster, /rest/v1/gradebook_results against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co with the publishable key from .env.example. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms the script returned 'ok: true' on 2026-04-25 and that the schema, seed, and smoke checks all loaded successfully.

site/index.html includes a copy-pasteable curl command against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/people with the publishable key (sb_publishable_DaJsnILCWdUIjl4cCaL3Jw_qLy8BPXK) inline in both the apikey and authorization headers. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md repeats the command. No env vars or non-public keys are required.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. scripts/evaluate_platform.py writes a per-rubric items array plus counts (pass/partial/fail/blocked/total) and an overall `done` flag, satisfying the per-item status array and overall summary requirements.

scripts/check_spec_conformance.py still runs in VERIFY.md and writes site/api/spec-conformance.json. scripts/codex_loop.py's `maybe_publish` function gates on `judge_result.publish_recommendation`, `after_eval.done`, and `rubric_progressed(before_eval, after_eval)` — i.e., the LLM evaluator output, not the advisory numeric score. The deterministic report is passed to the LLM evaluator's context (evaluate_platform.py loads ADVISORY_REPORT and includes it in the prompt as 'ADVISORY DETERMINISTIC REPORT (signal only, do not defer to it)').

scripts/codex_loop.py contains `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): print('Evaluator reports done after this iteration. Stopping the loop.'); return 0`. The `done` flag in evaluate_platform.py is True iff every item is `pass` or every non-pass is `blocked` with a documented prerequisite. Hard caps remain via --max-iterations (default 25), --stall-limit (default 3), and --codex-timeout-minutes safety rails.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
